name: Mexican Chocolate Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Line the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan with waxed paper.
2. Lightly grease the waxed paper and sides of the pan.
3. Melt the chocolate and let it cool to room temperature.
4. In a large bowl, beat the egg yolks at high speed.
5. Gradually add sugar and beat until light, about 4 minutes.
6. Add mayonnaise and beat for an additional minute.
7. Stir in the ground almonds and flour until blended.
8. Stir in Kahlua.
9. In a medium bowl, using clean beaters, beat egg whites until stiff.
10. Gently fold the egg white mixture into the batter.
11. Pour the batter into the prepared pan.
12. Bake at 350°F for 55 minutes or until firm.
13. Cool the cake in the pan on a wire rack for 10 minutes.
14. Remove from the pan and cool for an additional 30 minutes on a wire rack.
15. Invert the cake onto a wire rack, remove the bottom of the pan and waxed paper.
16. Allow to cool completely.
17. Sprinkle with confectioners' sugar. You may want to use a geometric pattern made from paper instead of the traditional doily design.
